text: The surgeon begins by dividing the gastric colic ligament along the distal transverse colon up to the hepatic flexure. The second portion of the duodenum is exposed, followed by the mobilization of the right colon through an incision along the white lines of Toldt. The hepatic flexure attachments are divided, allowing full mobilization of the hepatic flexure and exposure of the second portion of the duodenum and pancreatic head. Upward traction of the appendix facilitates dissection of the right colon from the right iliac fossa. A window is created lateral to the second portion of the duodenum, through which the transverse colon is pulled. An intracorporeal side-to-side anastomosis is performed.

During the laparoscopic procedure for lower rectal cancer, the surgeon performs a total mesorectal excision (TME) and Deloyer procedure. The transverse colon is rotated counterclockwise, positioning the cecum under the liver and the transverse colon toward the pelvis. The ischemic portion of the left colon is resected, and an anvil is inserted extracorporeally. An end-to-end anastomosis is completed using a CDH-XXI stapler.
